heading: Mandatory recall authority
label: 350l Mandatory recall authority

children.heading: Voluntary procedures
children.label: a Voluntary procedures
children.text: If the Secretary determines, based on information gathered through the reportable food registry under or through any other means, that there is a reasonable probability that an article of food (other than infant formula) is adulterated under or misbranded under and the use of or exposure to such article will cause serious adverse health consequences or death to humans or animals, the Secretary shall provide the responsible party (as defined in ) with an opportunity to cease distribution and recall such article.section 350f of this titlesection 342 of this titlesection 343(w) of this titlesection 350f of this title

children.heading: Prehearing order to cease distribution and give notice
children.label: b Prehearing order to cease distribution and give notice
A
children.text: immediately cease distribution of such article; and

children.label: i
children.text: manufacturing, processing, packing, transporting, distributing, receiving, holding, or importing and selling such article; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to which such article has been distributed, transported, or sold, to immediately cease distribution of such article.1So in original. The words “to immediately cease distribution of such article.” probably should follow cl. (ii).1

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If an article of food covered by a recall order issued under paragraph (1)(B) has been distributed to a warehouse-based third party logistics provider without providing such provider sufficient information to know or reasonably determine the precise identity of the article of food covered by a recall order that is in its possession, the notice provided by the responsible party subject to the order issued under paragraph (1)(B) shall include such information as is necessary for the warehouse-based third party logistics provider to identify the food.

children.label: i
children.text: to exempt a warehouse-based third party logistics provider from the requirements of this chapter, including the requirements in this section and ; orsection 350c of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: to exempt a warehouse-based third party logistics provider from being the subject of a mandatory recall order.

children.heading: Determination to limit areas affected
children.label: 3 Determination to limit areas affected
children.text: If the Secretary requires a responsible party to cease distribution under paragraph (1)(A) of an article of food identified in subsection (a), the Secretary may limit the size of the geographic area and the markets affected by such cessation if such limitation would not compromise the public health.

children.heading: Hearing on order
children.label: c Hearing on order
children.text: The Secretary shall provide the responsible party subject to an order under subsection (b) with an opportunity for an informal hearing, to be held as soon as possible, but not later than 2 days after the issuance of the order, on the actions required by the order and on why the article that is the subject of the order should not be recalled.

children.heading: Post-hearing recall order and modification of order
children.label: d Post-hearing recall order and modification of order
A
children.text: amend the order to require recall of such article or other appropriate action;

children.label: B
children.text: specify a timetable in which the recall shall occur;

children.label: C
children.text: require periodic reports to the Secretary describing the progress of the recall; and

children.label: D
children.text: provide notice to consumers to whom such article was, or may have been, distributed.

children.heading: Vacating of order
children.label: 2 Vacating of order
children.text: If, after such hearing, the Secretary determines that adequate grounds do not exist to continue the actions required by the order, or that such actions should be modified, the Secretary shall vacate the order or modify the order.

children.heading: Rule regarding alcoholic beverages
children.label: e Rule regarding alcoholic beverages
children.text: The Secretary shall not initiate a mandatory recall or take any other action under this section with respect to any alcohol beverage until the Secretary has provided the Alcohol and Tobacco Tax and Trade Bureau with a reasonable opportunity to cease distribution and recall such article under the Alcohol and Tobacco Tax and Trade Bureau authority.

children.heading: Cooperation and consultation
children.label: f Cooperation and consultation
children.text: The Secretary shall work with State and local public health officials in carrying out this section, as appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: of the recall to consumers and retailers to whom such article was, or may have been, distributed; and

children.label: i
children.text: the name of the article of food subject to the recall;

children.label: ii
children.text: a description of the risk associated with such article; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to the extent practicable, information for consumers about similar articles of food that are not affected by the recall;

consult the policies of the Department of Agriculture regarding providing to the public a list of retail consignees receiving products involved in a Class I recall and shall consider providing such a list to the public, as determined appropriate by the Secretary; and

if available, publish on the Internet Web site of the Food and Drug Administration an image of the article that is the subject of the press release described in (1).2So in original. Probably should be “paragraph (1).”2

children.heading: No delegation
children.label: h No delegation
children.text: The authority conferred by this section to order a recall or vacate a recall order shall not be delegated to any officer or employee other than the Commissioner.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: i Effect
children.text: Nothing in this section shall affect the authority of the Secretary to request or participate in a voluntary recall, or to issue an order to cease distribution or to recall under any other provision of this chapter or under the Public Health Service Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 201

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To assist in carrying out the requirements of this subsection, the Secretary shall establish an incident command operation or a similar operation within the Department of Health and Human Services that will operate not later than 24 hours after the initiation of a mandatory recall or the recall of an article of food for which the use of, or exposure to, such article will cause serious adverse health consequences or death to humans or animals.

children.label: A
children.text: ensure timely and coordinated communication within the Department, including enhanced communication and coordination between different agencies and organizations within the Department;

children.label: B
children.text: ensure timely and coordinated communication from the Department, including public statements, throughout the duration of the investigation and related foodborne illness outbreak;

children.label: C
children.text: identify a single point of contact within the Department for public inquiries regarding any actions by the Secretary related to a recall;

children.label: D
children.text: coordinate with Federal, State, local, and tribal authorities, as appropriate, that have responsibilities related to the recall of a food or a foodborne illness outbreak associated with a food that is subject to the recall, including notification of the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of Education in the event such recalled food is a commodity intended for use in a child nutrition program (as identified in ); andsection 1769f(b) of title 42

children.label: E
children.text: conclude operations at such time as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Multiple recalls
children.label: 3 Multiple recalls
children.text: The Secretary may establish multiple or concurrent incident command operations or similar operations in the event of multiple recalls or foodborne illness outbreaks necessitating such action by the Department of Health and Human Services.
